PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Nov-30-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Lily O'Toole (daughter) and Ora Meacham appearing via Zoom in propria persona.

Ms. O'Toole apologized for not appearing at the last court date as she was out of town for the hearing 2 weeks ago and stated that they have familiarized themselves with checking examiner's notes and the court's procedures. The Court asked if they knew that when they collect assets of the estate: they need to put them in the name of the estate, in insured accounts not more than $250,000.00 per bank until authorization for final distribution, and they cannot distribute assets until there is a court order. Ms. O'Toole and Ms. Meacham both stated they understood. The Court enquired what they have done to be familiar with rules of probate. Ms. O'Toole stated that along with her husband, they have looked at rules. Ms. Meacham stated she has studied rules 14 of probate administration to be familiarized with the process. The Court stated those are some of the rules but there are rules of statute and probate, that when they are self-represented they will have lots of expectations with the probate process while they are also in their grieving process, that there is a responsibility to protect the estate, and asked whether they still want to proceed without an attorney. Ms. O'Toole responded yes and stated that the main asset is real property. Ms. Meacham stated the plan is to follow her parents' wishes to keep the real property in the family, and that her parents worked very hard for that house. The Court stated that if they are not selling the property, it is inclined to grant the petition with limited authority, not requiring bond and the real property cannot be sold without a court approval. The court will appoint a probate referee (name TBD) to value estate assets. Status for filing inventory and appraisal set for March 8, 2021 for Inventory and Appraisal to be filed one week prior. Status on close of estate is set for November 8, 2021 for a petition for final distribution or petition for approval of report on status of administration to be filed by November 1, 2021. The petition for letters of administration is taken under submission. The Court instructs petitioners to watch the register of action for filed order to get letters issued.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: S. Le. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Nov-04-2020 Continued To Nov-18-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ora Meacham-Williams, Petitioner in pro per (appearing by video conference).

The Court notes the absence of Lily O'Toole. Ms. Meacham-Williams reports that Ms. O'Toole's plane flight was delayed and unfortunately, she will not arrive until this afternoon. The Court reports that in the absence of Ms. O'Toole the hearing cannot proceed as all Petitioners must be present for the appointment hearing particularly where, as here, there are issues.

After brief discussion, the Court continues the hearing to November 18, 2020 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Deputy: K. Kabasares; Reporter: Not Reported. (D204)
